findings.title: Registry reconciliation does not cover the full PhaseContract encoding
findings.detail: Concern C4 and Task 4 only call out updates to requiredInputs, producedOutputs, advance_event, gatedDecision, and terminal_reason. The acceptance criteria require PhaseContract to losslessly encode all six phase-semantics roles, which also depends on routing and delegation fields such as next_action, gated, terminal, next_phase, gated_event_kind, gated_event_type, agent, agentTask, and cliCommands, including explicit empty-set encodings for terminal and purely agent-delegated phases. Expand the reconciliation and verification steps so every encoding field that carries branching or delegation semantics is audited and corrected.

findings.title: No explicit source-of-truth audit for phases and vocabulary
findings.detail: The design says the phase set must come from canonical-workflow-state and every referenced artifact, event, and run-state term must already exist in an owning capability, but the tasks only say to enumerate 21 phases and draft definitions. That leaves no concrete step to derive the phase list from canonical-workflow-state or to verify each cited term against artifact-ownership-model, surface-event-contract, workflow-run-state, and related capabilities. Add an explicit verification task for phase-set parity and vocabulary resolution before finalizing phase-semantics text.

findings.title: Accepted Spec Conflicts fallback is underspecified
findings.detail: The proposal allows unresolved divergences only when design.md records them with both justification and a follow-up change reference, but the design's Accepted Spec Conflicts table schema has no follow-up field and Task 4.3 asks only for rationale. Task 5.3 includes follow-up references, so the fallback process is inconsistent across workstreams. Update the section schema and both reconciliation task groups so every accepted conflict captures the required follow-up reference as well as the rationale.

findings.title: Slash-command reconciliation is scoped narrower than the spec allows
findings.detail: Concern C5 and Tasks 5.1-5.4 only audit slash-command prose for inputs, outputs, branching, and delegation boundary, and the risk/task language permits larger prose discrepancies to be deferred as Accepted Spec Conflicts because they are large. The proposal requires command templates to be corrected for any disagreement with `phase-semantics` across the six mandatory roles, including completion condition, unless the mismatch truly cannot be reconciled in this change. Expand the prose-reconciliation scope to cover completion-condition drift as well, and reserve Accepted Spec Conflicts for genuinely unreconcilable mismatches rather than paragraph-sized but fixable edits.
